section.heading: Preservation education and training program
section.chapeau: The Secretary, in consultation with the Council and other appropriate Federal, tribal, Native Hawaiian, and non-Federal organizations, shall develop and implement a comprehensive preservation education and training program. The program shall include—

paragraph.content: standards and increased preservation training opportunities for Federal workers involved in preservation-related functions;
preservation training opportunities for other Federal, State, tribal and local government workers, and students;

technical or financial assistance, or both, to historically black colleges and universities, to tribal colleges, and to colleges with a high enrollment of Native Americans or Native Hawaiians, to establish preservation training and degree programs; and

paragraph.chapeau: where appropriate, coordination with the National Center for Preservation Technology and Training of—

subparagraph.content: distribution of information on preservation technologies;
provision of training and skill development in trades, crafts, and disciplines related to historic preservation in Federal training and development programs; and
support for research, analysis, conservation, curation, interpretation, and display related to preservation.

html:tr.style: font-size:6pt; border-bottom:1px solid black; border-top:1px solid black; -uslm-lc:h1;

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;
width:68.0pt ; max-width:68.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black;
width:94.0pt ; max-width:94.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-left:1px solid black;

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;
html:td.html:p: Pub. L. 89–665, title I, § 101(j), as added Pub. L. 102–575, title XL, § 4008, Oct. 30, 1992, 106 Stat. 4758.

note.p: In paragraph (1), the word “new” is omitted as unnecessary.
In paragraph (2), the word “increased” is omitted as unnecessary.
